title: ophrys bertolonii subsp. aurelia
intro: Ophrys bertolonii subsp. aurelia, also known as the Golden Spotted Bee Orchid, is a type of orchid plant that belongs to the Ophrys genus. It is a rare and exotic species found in the Mediterranean region, known for its distinctive golden and brown markings, which resemble a female bee. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its rich content of saponins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Ophrys bertolonii subsp. aurelia can be utilized through herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, offering health benefits like relieving digestive discomfort, reducing inflammation, and supporting the immune system.
